name: Pompano in Paper
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Ripe Olives, Pitted and Chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Use fresh or frozen Pompano or other fish fillets in this recipe.
2. Drain and chop the pimentos; use about 1/4 cup.
3. Thaw fish fillets if frozen.
4. Cut parchment paper or brown paper into 6 squares, each 12 inches.
5. Place one fillet on half of each piece of parchment paper and sprinkle with a little salt.
6. In a saucepan, cook onion and annatto seed in oil until the onion is tender.
7. Stir in orange juice, lemon juice, pimentos, olives, and parsley.
8. Simmer, covered, for about 5 minutes.
9. Add chopped hard cooked eggs to the mixture.
10. Spoon about 3 tablespoons of the sauce over each fillet.
11. Fold parchment paper over the fillet and seal by turning edges up and folding.
12. Place packets in a baking pan measuring 15 inches long, 10 inches wide, and 1 inch high.
13. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 20 to 25 minutes or until fish is cooked through.
14. Cut packets open with a large X on top; fold back each segment.
15. Transfer packets to dinner plates. Garnish with shredded lettuce and sliced radishes if desired.
